title: System
description: A type-level complex system in the catalog. v0.2 ‚Äî adds optional periodic-table fields (system_kind, substrate, origin, primary_function, main_feedbacks, dominant_constraints, emergent_properties, failure_modes, lifecycle_stage, priority) sourced from docs/framework/01-discovery-framework.md ¬ß5 and docs/framework/02-candidate-systems-catalog.md ¬ß27. New fields are all optional so v0.1 records remain valid.

status.description: Lifecycle state. `bootstrap-stub` (added in v0.2) marks records imported in bulk from docs/framework/02-candidate-systems-catalog.md with placeholder boundary/components/scales ‚Äî usable as catalog index but not yet substantive. `candidate` is a hand- or scout-authored entry awaiting review. `profiling` is in-progress upgrade. `profiled` is review-validated. `deprecated` is retained for history.

priority.description: Curatorial priority tag from docs/framework/02-candidate-systems-catalog.md ¬ß2: P0 foundational, P1 core, P2 specialized/applied, P3 abstract/model, C control/boundary case. Optional; absent means unranked.

system_kind.description: What kind of object this entry represents. `class` (default for most catalog entries) ‚Äî recurring archetype. `transition` ‚Äî emergence transition (e.g. multicellularity, language). `subsystem` ‚Äî nested system primarily of interest as a component (rare; usually classes). `model` ‚Äî abstract/computational model system (cellular automata, Ising). `boundary_case` ‚Äî anti-example used to calibrate metric space (simple pendulum, ideal gas). See docs/framework/02-candidate-systems-catalog.md ¬ß1 and ¬ß25.

substrate.description: Free-text description of what the system is made of: matter, energy, organisms, humans, institutions, information, software, infrastructure, hybrid, etc. From docs/framework/02 ¬ß29. Single string rather than array ‚Äî most systems have one dominant substrate; for hybrids combine into one descriptor (e.g. "humans + infrastructure + institutions").

origin.description: How the system came into being. `natural` ‚Äî physical-law assembly (atoms, planets). `evolved` ‚Äî biological/cultural evolution. `designed` ‚Äî intentional engineering (power grid, software). `emergent` ‚Äî arises from collective dynamics without designer (markets, language). `hybrid` ‚Äî multiple origins combined.

boundary_clarity.description: Independent of `boundary.type`: how sharp the inside/outside distinction is. From docs/framework/01 ¬ß10.1, ¬ß5.1.

primary_function.description: What the system does or maintains, in one sentence. Useful for cross-domain analogy queries ("what other systems do X?").

lifecycle_stage.description: Where the type-level archetype typically sits in its life cycle. For most enduring archetypes this is `maturity`; transition entries (multicellularity, agriculture) may be `formation`. From docs/framework/01 ¬ß4.8.

main_feedbacks.description: Salient feedback loops ‚Äî positive/reinforcing and negative/balancing. Free-text descriptions; the typology is in docs/framework/01 ¬ß4.16, ¬ß5.4.

dominant_constraints.description: What limits the system's possible behavior: physical, energetic, informational, ecological, legal, social, computational, economic. From docs/framework/03 ¬ß13.

emergent_properties.description: Macro-level patterns not present at component level. From docs/framework/01 ¬ß4.7, ¬ß5.2.

failure_modes.description: How the system tends to break. Use the failure-family vocabulary in docs/framework/01 ¬ß4.4 and the taxonomy in docs/framework/03 ¬ß19 where possible (cascade, fragmentation, runaway-feedback, capture, etc.).

primary_resources.description: What the system consumes/transforms to maintain function: energy, matter, information, attention, computation, capital, labor, trust, legitimacy, time, coordination bandwidth. From docs/framework/01 ¬ß4.11, ¬ß5.6.

source_refs.description: Optional. Type-level system entries (the catalog's primary unit) generally do not require source citation ‚Äî `cells exist` is canonical knowledge. Cite when the entry's prose makes a specific quantitative or contested claim that should be grounded. Metric and observation records still require sources.

canonical_examples.description: Concrete instances of this system type, listed as exemplars (not as separate catalog entries). E.g. for `multicellular-organism`: C. elegans, Drosophila, mouse, human. Recommended >=3 examples per type-level entry; the inclusion criterion in AGENTS.md treats `has at least 3 well-known instances` as a quality gate. Not required by schema so genuinely-singular types (`the-internet`, `earth-system`) remain valid.
